title: bus.intrinsic.marker v1
description: Ecosystem health and lifecycle marker. Published by any project at structurally significant moments — cycle start/complete, build, test, deploy, error spikes, resource breaches, and quality signals. Consumers (sysmap, deer-flow auditor, hearth dashboard) use these to track project health without parsing raw lifecycle events. `project` is the canonical project slug (e.g. 'hearth', 'hearth-loom', 'deer-flow'). `marker_type` is the event kind; `phase` is the human-readable workflow stage. `linked_id` ties the marker to its parent artifact (bead, session, commit, cycle). `outcome` is only required on terminal markers (cycle.complete, build.complete, etc.). Resource costs are optional and carry only the incremental cost for this marker's work unit.

project.description: Canonical project slug (e.g. 'hearth', 'hearth-loom', 'deer-flow', 'tengine', 'nervous-bus', 'autobench'). Must match the sysmap KNOWN_PROJECTS slug or a registered project slug.

marker_type.description: Category of marker. 'agent.cycle.start' and 'agent.cycle.complete' bracket a unit of agent work (bead, research cycle, council). 'build.complete' fires after a successful or failed build. 'test.run' after a CI or local test pass/fail. 'deploy.complete' when a service or binary is deployed. 'error.spike' when error rate crosses a threshold. 'resource.breach' when CPU/memory/GPU exceeds contract. 'quality.signal' for AC-pass, score improvements, or degradations.

phase.description: Workflow stage the project is in at the time of this marker. 'planning' — design/triage. 'coding' — active agent edits. 'testing' — unit/integration/AC checks. 'gates' — PR review + CI gating. 'deploy' — binary push / service restart. 'recycle' — post-deploy observation, issue filing, next cycle preparation.

linked_id.description: The artifact this marker is anchored to: bead_id (e.g. 'hearth-vkm4'), session_id (ULID), commit SHA (40 hex chars), or cycle_id. Allows consumers to join markers back to the originating event chain.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the marker was produced. Mirrors the envelope 'time' field for consumers reading the flat data payload.

duration_ms.description: Optional elapsed time in milliseconds from the paired start marker to this marker. Present on terminal markers (cycle.complete, build.complete, test.run, deploy.complete) when the start was also marked.

outcome.description: Terminal disposition of the work unit. Required on terminal marker_types (agent.cycle.complete, build.complete, test.run, deploy.complete). 'accepted' — work merged and deployed. 'rework' — sent back for iteration. 'reverted' — merged then reverted. 'failed' — hard failure, no merge. 'partial' — partial completion (some tests/stages passed).

quality_score.description: Optional normalized quality score [0.0, 1.0] at the time of this marker. For agent cycles: AC pass rate or deer-flow score_after. For builds/tests: pass fraction. Consumers use this to build project health trends.

resource_cost.description: Optional incremental resource consumption for this work unit. Omit fields that are not applicable.

tokens.description: LLM tokens consumed (input + output combined) during this work unit.

compute_ms.description: Wall-clock CPU time in milliseconds consumed by non-GPU compute during this work unit.

gpu_ms.description: GPU compute time in milliseconds during this work unit. For inference and training jobs.
